subject: [OOF] CMSO logs to STDOUT
commit_message:
[OOF] CMSO logs to STDOUT

Root level change to INFO.
Result should be more logs in STDOUT.

Issue-ID: OOM-2514
Signed-off-by: Dominik Mizyn <d.mizyn@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: Ie64f0aff59a732f4a798e0a27d7ae18e38dfe2e2
[Add VM_ARGS define to point to the right logback file]
Suggested-by: Krishna Moorthy <krishna.moorthy6@wipro.com>
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Opasiak <k.opasiak@samsung.com>
Signed-off-by: Krishna Moorthy <krishna.moorthy6@wipro.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

all logs from pods are almost empty
@krishna, is it normal?

by the way, what's "mso-simulator" pod? (https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113414-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-oof-cmso-service-7cd4c5c949-kjlqb-mso-simulator-logs.html)
Are you sure we need to deploy it?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> all logs from pods are almost empty
> @krishna, is it normal?
> 
> by the way, what's "mso-simulator" pod? (https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113414-1/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-oof-cmso-service-7cd4c5c949-kjlqb-mso-simulator-logs.html)
> Are you sure we need to deploy it?

I have to check with the developers who contributed the code to CMSO. They are currently not active in the community.

I beleive the simulator is mocking the SO and it has some robot test suites of CMSO. Since no use case is currently supporting it, the integration with SO is not done and there is no contributions to support it.

We can remove the simulator and try pointing it to the real SO, assuming that it will not break any integration test suites. It should work if there are no tests running for CMSO.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> @Krishna
> any hints how to force those components to log to stdout?

I once had a look at it before. Seems the logback file that is being mounted from the charts are not properly fed to the application. The application uses an internal logback file within the image. I will check to see if it could be overridden by this logback or we need remove the logback from the image and try.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > @Krishna
> > any hints how to force those components to log to stdout?
> 
> I once had a look at it before. Seems the logback file that is being mounted from the charts are not properly fed to the application. The application uses an internal logback file within the image. I will check to see if it could be overridden by this logback or we need remove the logback from the image and try.

we can override it that's not an issue. Just give us a path where oof expects those to be

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 2:
> > > 
> > > @Krishna
> > > any hints how to force those components to log to stdout?
> > 
> > I once had a look at it before. Seems the logback file that is being mounted from the charts are not properly fed to the application. The application uses an internal logback file within the image. I will check to see if it could be overridden by this logback or we need remove the logback from the image and try.
> 
> we can override it that's not an issue. Just give us a path where oof expects those to be

Currently, the logback is not configured anywhere. It is fetching the logback from the classpath by default which is hardcoded within the jar file. There is an env VM_ARGS which is being used by the startup script where we can pass any additional arguments to the application. We can pass the following argument "-Dcom.att.eelf.logging.file" to specify the logback file

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

OK for me

> > 
> > we can override it that's not an issue. Just give us a path where oof expects those to be
> 
> Currently, the logback is not configured anywhere. It is fetching the logback from the classpath by default which is hardcoded within the jar file. There is an env VM_ARGS which is being used by the startup script where we can pass any additional arguments to the application. We can pass the following argument "-Dcom.att.eelf.logging.file" to specify the logback file

Ok this sounds good. Worth trying. I'll update the patch shortly

It seems that the command line is already there but for some reason logs are empty. Any idea?

> It seems that the command line is already there but for some reason logs are empty. Any idea?

No, I guess it's still picking the one in the image. Let me create a patch to remove the logback.xml from the classpath, update this patch with a snapshot/staging image. If it works, then I will release the image and update it here as well.

Does that sound okay?

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> I just see logs before tomcat starts on some:
> https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113414-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-oof-cmso-topology-5c44ff6797-qnp6z-oof-cmso-topology-logs.html
> https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113414-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-oof-cmso-ticketmgt-5b78895695-vkwsz-oof-cmso-ticketmgt-logs.html
> 
> but not on others so I guess it's OK.
> 
> I still  have a question:
> what is mso simulator in OOF CMSO service? https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113414-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-oof-cmso-service-67d646d869-wgq8m.html
> 
> do we really need it?

Yes. I checked with one of the developer who worked in CMSO. CMSO was originally introduced as part of the change management use case, CMSO was planned to be integrated with SO. But it seems that it never took off. So, for the testing they used a simulator and included it in the helm charts too, for running some e2e testing.

Removing that side car container should not create any problem. Can you create a jira for me? I will take care of it.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> oh and it seems truststore has issue, looking at the logs:
> https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//113414-5/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-oof-cmso-ticketmgt-5b78895695-vkwsz-oof-cmso-ticketmgt-logs.html
> 
> 2021-03-30 09:44:14.451  WARN 8 --- [           main] org.apache.tomcat.util.net.SSLUtilBase   : jsseUtil.trustedCertNotValid

I see. But the healthcheck is passing. The healthcheck api in the cmso-service will call the healthcheck apis of other components and returns a consolidated healthcheck result. So, I assume that the interaction between the CMSO components are working fine and cmso-service must be using the truststore to send the healthcheck request. Let me check further about the warning message

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+2

results looks good enough but as I got my hands dirty with this patch I'd let @Sylvain to make the final review and merge it

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

we can see the logs from all CSMO pods
the CSMO HC tests remain PASS and are the only tests impacted by this component
Would probably make sense to integrate a smoke test dealing with OOF in the future
@krishna, if you have already a End to End scenario in mind, it would be interesting to discuss what woudl be relevant to cover the component with a basic End to End test

we got errors in gating
is seems that the SDC took time to start
healthdist artifacts is false but after 1 retry (10 minutes later) it shall be OK because the results in the DB is PASS (that what we see in the dashboard that take the last result corresponding to this test - basically the job have natively a retry mechanism, but  in CI we save only the artifacts of the first try)
http://testresults.opnfv.org/onap/api/v1/results?build_tag=gitlab_ci-functest-kubespray-baremetal-daily-master-113414-6&case_name=healthdist

And apparently there is an issue in basic_onboard when there is no service certified (usually healthdist is done and the list is not empty) so it looks like a bug on pythonsdk, I will create a JIRA

and for the E2E test, basic_cnf reached the SO timeout

Yes, Morgan. The existing SON use case testsuite will cover one of the apps in OOF. We just need to add it to the gating.

https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=testsuite.git;a=blob;f=robot/testsuites/usecases/5gson.robot;h=e8382883c28507cb27e06bcd8eecea34a4be0216;hb=refs/heads/master

We can add some e2e tests for Network Slicing usecase which will cover many functionalities in OOF. We can discuss with the use case team to take it forward
